title: Bound retries and alert on exhaustion
description: Configure finite retries with backoff for transient faults and route final failures to a notification so they are not lost.
body: Use when a task can fail from transient causes (network, rate limits). Do not retry infinitely or retry non-transient errors — infinite retries hide real breakage and waste resources. Actions: set a bounded retry count with exponential backoff for transient failures, fail fast on deterministic errors, and wire a notification on final failure with run and task context. Validation signals include a task that recovers within the retry budget on a transient fault and an alert delivered when retries are exhausted. Risk boundary: silent retry loops mask outages and delay response; if a task exhausts retries, treat it as a real incident, notify, and escalate rather than auto-resetting it.
